text.title: Rust Unit Tests
text.summary: Co-located unit tests with mockall, async test patterns via #[tokio::test], and assert_matches assertions.

rule.statement: Place unit tests in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in the same file as the SUT. Use `tests/*.rs` only for integration tests against the public API.
rule.rationale: Co-located tests have direct access to private functions and types. The fastest dev loop keeps test code next to the code under test.

rule.statement: Pin the tokio runtime flavor explicitly: `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` for code that calls `spawn_blocking`.
rule.rationale: `current_thread` flavor deadlocks if the SUT spawns a blocking task. Explicit flavor prevents silent runtime behavior differences between test and production code.

rule.statement: Always `await` joined async task handles. `tokio::spawn` panics are silent unless the handle is awaited.
rule.rationale: A background task panicking with the handle dropped causes tests to pass while actual logic failed. This is the single most confusing Rust async test failure mode.

example.note: `assert_matches!` provides pattern-matching assertions that give meaningful failure messages. `times(1)` verifies the mock is called exactly once.

rule.statement: Prefer a simple in-memory fake implementation over mockall when the trait has >5 methods or state-dependent behavior.
rule.rationale: mockall-generated mocks with complex expectations become harder to read than the production code. A 30-line `FakeDb` is more maintainable than 30 mock expectations.
